notes: 12 fit modules (one per stage) — FIT-ONLY speed campaign, no fight, no split. Speed-specific fields: warmup_gate (every module opens with a MANDATORY ~15-min neuromuscular/dynamic warm-up that must be completed before high-intensity work unlocks); foot_contact_cap + set_reps + rest_sec on plyometric modules (NEVER above the beginner cap); sprint_session {reps, distance_m, recovery} on sprint modules (FULL recovery, never traded away); pillar tags the speed quality trained. Numbers shown are the NORMAL 'GOTTA GO FAST' baseline; Easy/Hard scale via campaign.difficulty_scaling. SAFETY (encoded from the report): warm-up is not optional; plyo foot-contacts capped (beginner 60-80), sets of 5-10, >=1 min rest, 48h between plyo (24h ok for low-amplitude); max-velocity sprints low-volume/high-quality with full recovery; land soft with bent knees (never 'stick'); ankle-first parkour, master landing/roll before any jump, NO flips; if pain occurs cut foot-contacts 50%; recovery = sleep/nutrition/hydration. Every module stops on any pain flag or safety stop criterion.

modules.title: Warm-Up Gate & Running Form
exercises: NEUROMUSCULAR WARM-UP (~15 min, FIFA 11+ style): joint mobility, glute/core activation, dynamic stretch, progressive-intensity running
Running-form drills: A-marches, A-skips, wall drives (light)
modules.coach_note: Teach the warm-up as non-negotiable — it lowers injury AND makes you faster. No maximal efforts this stage; it's form + habit.

modules.title: Land & Stabilize (Ankle-First)
exercises: Soft landing practice: drop to a soft, bent-knee, quiet landing from a low step — absorb, never 'stick'
Safety roll from a squat (both sides)
Single-leg balance + ankle stability (eyes open -> eyes closed)
modules.coach_note: Master the soft landing and roll BEFORE any real jumps — the ankle is the highest-risk joint. Land quiet = land safe. No height progression until landings are clean.

modules.title: Build the Base
exercises: Easy conversational run (able to talk throughout)
A few relaxed pickups (build to ~75%, not max)
Nordic curl / eccentric hamstring (assisted, low reps)

run_guidance.volume_ramp: increase weekly volume by no more than ~10% (conservative convention)

modules.coach_note: Base runs are EASY on purpose — the aerobic engine is built slow. Nordic curls condition the hamstring (an independent injury-risk factor). Watch for shin pain; ramp volume gently.

modules.title: First Hops (Low-Intensity Plyo)
modules.coach_note: Count foot-contacts and STOP at the cap. Sets of 5-10 with a full minute rest. Land soft and quiet every rep. Low-amplitude work can go every ~24h; if anything pinches, cut contacts by half.

modules.title: Off the Line (Acceleration)
exercises: Short accelerations (build to max over the distance)
modules.coach_note: Acceleration is about clean, powerful mechanics — low drive-out, patient posture rise. Full recovery between reps; stop when form frays. This is quality, not conditioning.

modules.title: Quick Feet (Agility & Footwork)

agility.cue: decelerate under control before every cut

modules.coach_note: The cut is the risky moment — sink the hips, control the deceleration, then change direction. Eyes up, feet quick. Parameters are conservative convention (agility dosing wasn't fixed by the research).

modules.title: Spring Loaded (Plyo Progression)
exercises: Broad jumps (stick the landing soft, reset each rep)
Optional: single-leg hops (only once bilateral is clean)
modules.coach_note: Intensity up, discipline the same: cap the contacts, 48h since your last plyo session, bilateral before single-leg. Every landing soft. Broad jumps reset fully between reps — no rebounding while fatigued.

modules.title: Top Speed (Max-Velocity Sprints)

sprint_session.recovery: FULL between every rep (do not shortcut)

exercises: Max-velocity sprints: 40-60m, last portion at top speed
Full recovery walk-back + reset between every rep
modules.coach_note: The fastest running in the campaign — and the most demanding on hamstrings. 3-4 reps only, FULL recovery every time, stop while reps are still fast. Any sharp posterior-thigh twinge = stop immediately.

modules.title: Free Runner (Parkour Fundamentals)

parkour.rule: master landing + roll before any jump; start low with cleared landings; master before adding height

exercises: Low precision jumps (land exactly, within a height you can land perfectly)
Two-handed vault over a low obstacle
modules.coach_note: Ankle-first, always. Cleared landing zone, soft absorption, master the roll. Keep jump heights to what you can land perfectly — 'master before progressing,' never a rep-count target (that scheme was refuted). Absolutely NO flips.

modules.title: Speed Combine (All Pillars)

plyo.note: small finisher only — combine day keeps plyo volume light

modules.coach_note: Rehearsal for the finale — sequence the skills while fresh, respect every cap, keep quality high. Do the hard efforts first (accel), accessories after.

modules.title: Recovery Zone (Deload)
rules: Deliberately easy — this is recovery, not a session to grind
No max efforts, no plyo, no sprinting
exercises: Easy conversational jog or brisk walk
Ankle stability + light eccentric hamstring (low reps)
modules.coach_note: The back-off that makes the finale fast and safe. Completing it is REWARDED — do not skip it or turn it into a workout. Recovery rests on sleep, food, and water, not gadgets.

modules.title: The Blue Blur (Final Gauntlet)
exercises: Max-velocity sprint set (40-60m x 3, FULL recovery, capped volume)
rules: Quality-capped and warm-up gated — hit fast, clean reps and STOP at the cap.
NEVER to exhaustion or failure. Enter rested after the Stage 11 deload.
Full recovery between max efforts is non-negotiable.
modules.coach_note: The finale is quality at speed — everything you built, capped and controlled. Fast, clean, full recovery, then stop in control. Not a test of exhaustion.
